What kind of liability do Partners have under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004?
column4: "Under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004, each Partner is liable jointly and severally with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while he is a Partner, unless otherwise agreed by all the other Partners."
column8: "Каждый партнер несет солидарную ответственность за все долги и обязательства общего партнерства, возникшие в период его участия, если иное не согласовано всеми остальными партнерами."
column10: "Each Partner is liable jointly and severally for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while he is a Partner."

column2: Under the Foundations Law DIFC Law No. 3 of 2018, what is the maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within 30 days?
column4: "The maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within 30 days is $1,500. This is based on Article 10(7) of the Foundations Law, which specifies a fine for a person failing to provide a copy of assignment of rights."

column2: Under the Operating Law - DIFC Law No. 7 of 2018, who is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar?
column4: "Under the Operating Law, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar. Specifically, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA shall appoint an individual to serve as the Registrar and may dismiss such a person from the office of Registrar. However, prior to appointing or dismissing the Registrar, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA must consult the President."
column8: "Ответственность за назначение и увольнение Регистратора несет Совет директоров DIFCA, который должен консультироваться с Президентом перед принятием решения."

According to the Conclusion section of case CA 009/2024, how did the Court of Appeal rule, and what costs were awarded?
column4: "According to the Conclusion section of case CA 009/2024, the Court of Appeal allowed the appeal in part. It restored the Second Part 50 order in its amended form against Ms Onita and dismissed the appeal against the Order insofar as it related to Mr Oron. The Court accepted the second ground of appeal, finding the Judge was wrong in his approach to and interpretation of the rule and in finding Ms Onita was not an Officer. Regarding costs, the Appellant succeeded substantially in establishing the principles and the Judge's error concerning Ms Onita but failed regarding the other target. Consequently, the Appellant is in principle entitled to its costs with a measure of discount, as there is a substantial disparity in the costs claimed by the parties."
column8: "The Court of Appeal ruled to allow the Appeal to the extent ordered. The Judge had previously made no order as to costs, which was contested by the Defendant. The appeal on costs was partially granted, but specific costs awarded are not detailed in the provided context."

What is the minimum period for which a DIFC-incorporated General Partnership and a DIFC-incorporated Limited Liability Partnership must preserve their accounting records?
column4: "Based on the context, a DIFC-incorporated General Partnership must preserve its accounting records for at least six (6) years from the date of their creation, as per Article 18(2)(b) of the General Partnership Law. Similarly, a DIFC-incorporated Limited Liability Partnership must preserve its accounting records for at least six (6) years from the date of their creation, as per Article 25(3)(b) of the Limited Liability Partnership Law. Both laws specify this as the minimum period, subject to any other period that may be prescribed in the Regulations."

column2: What is the purpose of Article 9(1) of the Foundations Law?
column4: "Article 9(1) of the Foundations Law grants the Board of Directors of the DIFCA the authority to make Regulations to facilitate the administration of, or further the objects of, this Law."
column8: "Цель статьи 9(1) Закона о Фондах заключается в том, чтобы иностранный фонд, желающий вести операции в DIFC, мог подать заявку на получение лицензии как признанный иностранный фонд в соответствии с этим законом."
